title: Explaining Culture to Technology, Paul Ford | Compile 26
summary: Paul Ford discusses what technologists can learn from magazine production and proposes that culture is fundamentally a distributed, lossy prediction model rather than merely its output. He explores connections between culture, consciousness, and rhetoric, framing culture as an operating system for society.
writeup: Paul Ford's Compile 26 talk reframes culture as a distributed, lossy prediction model rather than just the artifacts it produces, drawing lessons from magazine production for technologists. The argument connects culture to consciousness and rhetoric, casting culture as an operating system for society. The framing matters for builders accustomed to thinking in system architecture: it treats shared cultural prediction as infrastructure, not output.
